articles.title: What is 'pink slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?
articles.description: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.
articles.content:
Experts say a series of AI-generated online news outlets posing as local mastheads in regional Western Australia could be the first recorded example of "pink slime" journalism in Australia.
"Pink slime" is the colloquial term for AI-generated news si... [4349 chars]

articles.title: Spotify’s new AI app can generate daily briefings and personalized podcasts for you
articles.description: Spotify's new Studio app uses AI to generate personal podcasts and daily briefings built around your listening history, calendar, and inbox.
articles.content: A month ago, Spotify let you build a podcast playlist with just a prompt. Now it wants to skip the playlist entirely and generate the podcast itself. Studio by Spotify Labs is a new standalone desktop app that uses AI to create personal podcasts, dai... [1627 chars]

articles.title: You Can Now Add ChatGPT To PowerPoint
articles.description: Most users can start generating slides with AI today.
articles.content: OpenAI is now letting you give your presentations the AI treatment. The company announced that ChatGPT is available in Microsoft PowerPoint. The chatbot can create new presentation slides, as well as editing and updating existing ones. As with many o... [852 chars]

articles.title: The Magnificent 7 Trade Is Starting To Break Apart
articles.description: The Magnificent 7 trade is breaking apart as AI capex, index concentration, and structural alpha reshape where investors should look for the next market winners.
articles.content:
For the last few years, investors have been able to hide behind one trade. Own the Magnificent 7. Own the index. Own the future.

articles.title: New York state lawmakers take aim at House AI talks
articles.description: Two Democratic state lawmakers from New York are the latest to urge their congressional counterparts to oppose negotiations that could block states from passing new AI safety laws.
articles.content: “The most recent negotiations over preemption are another attempt to paper over a fundamentally rotten idea: that the federal government should stop states from acting while Congress itself fails to implement a serious, multi-faceted, comprehensive a... [1810 chars]

articles.title: Machine learning personalizes depression treatment with the help of wearable technology
articles.description: More than 21% of U.S. adults experience depression, greatly impacting their quality of life. Many people with mild-to-moderate depression can improve their symptoms by adjusting daily habits like sleep, exercise, diet and social interaction, according to Jyoti Mishra, Ph.D., associate professor of psychiatry at University of California San Diego School of Medicine. However, because depression is highly variable between people, a one-size-fits-all lifestyle approach isn't very effective.

articles.description: The federal government and several provincial governments are turning to artificial intelligence to analyze laws and search for outdated regulations as they try to cut back on red tape.

articles.title: Spotify and Universal Launch AI Remix Tool for Fan
articles.description: Spotify and Universal Music Group say their new licensed AI remix platform will let fans create song covers and remixes while compensating participating artists and songwriters.
articles.content:
Taylor Swift fans may soon be able to legally generate AI remixes of her songs on Spotify.
